question.label: Growth blockers
question.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
question.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

answer: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
note: He names a specific blocker to next-level scope: staying in execution details is strongly limiting growth, while his self-diagnosis shows some clarity about the gap.

question.label: Stretch now
question.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?
question.description: Distinguishes comfort-zone execution from genuine growth moments.

answer: When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
note: He names a specific stretch point — political conversations — and describes retreating into direct delivery, signaling a strong growth constraint and mild disengagement under influence-heavy conditions.

question.label: Political conversations
question.name: In those political conversations, what makes you pull back from the room?
question.description: Drills into the specific dynamic behind his retreat from influence-heavy situations.

answer: I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
note: He names a specific growth gap: he can deliver projects but hesitates when the work shifts to cross-org alignment and influence.

question.label: Cross-org alignment
question.name: Cross-org alignment — what about that work makes you hesitate?
question.description: Drills into the specific fear or constraint behind his hesitation in influence-heavy work.

answer: Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
note: He names a confidence gap in senior forums as the reason he hesitates in cross-org alignment, a strong growth constraint; [THREAD-DEFERRED] confidence in senior forums should be picked up next session because drill cap requires stage progression.

question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

answer: I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
note: He names a specific self-limiting pattern — not being explicit about desired opportunities — which signals stalled growth and mildly reduced agency; also implies a clarity issue off-signature.

question.label: Opportunity ask
question.name: What opportunities have you wanted but not named out loud?
question.description: Drills into the specific opportunities he has not been explicit about so aspiration becomes concrete.

answer: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
note: He names a concrete aspiration — owning a strategic bet with visible stakeholders — which is a strong positive growth signal; no wellbeing signal was given.

question.label: Strategic bet gap
question.name: What stands between you and owning a strategic bet today?
question.description: Surfaces the main gap between his current role and the strategic ownership he wants.

answer: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
note: He names delegation of hands-on work as the main constraint, which shows a clear gap to strategic ownership with mild growth drag and capacity pressure. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'delegate more of the hands-on work' is a concrete thread to pick up next session.

question.label: Executor mode
question.name: Where are you still operating more like an executor than a strategic owner?
question.description: Identifies the parts of his work that remain tactical when they may need broader ownership.

answer: A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
note: Names a missing structure — a clear growth contract with milestones — which signals a strong growth investment gap and some clarity about what would help.

question.label: Next move
question.name: What is the first concrete move you want to make before we next meet?
question.description: Closes on a specific commitment that advances the growth plan without opening a new topic.

note: [SKIP] no signal — planner bypassed, queue carried forward
